title: Verbs Related to Cinema and Theater

senses.definition: to judge the value or importance of something

examples.x: The company rates its customer service very highly.
I rate his work very highly because he's so talented.
The movie was rated poorly by critics.
He rates the idea of going on vacation very highly.
The restaurant was rated highly for its delicious food.

senses.definition: to play or perform a role in a play, movie, etc.

examples.x: She acted the role of Juliet beautifully in the school play.

senses.definition: ‌to say something in a speech or a performance that you have not prepared or practiced
to have a leading role alongside another actor in a movie, TV show, or play
to act a role in an exaggerated way that is not natural

examples.x: He tends to overact by emphasizing every line with dramatic gestures.
The comedian intentionally overacts to elicit laughs from the audience.
During rehearsals, he was overacting until the director intervened.
Critics have noticed that he has overacted in previous roles.
If he continues to overact, he will struggle to convey the character's true emotions.

senses.definition: to represent the role of a particular character in a play, movie, etc.

examples.x: He played a villain in the action movie.
She had always desired to play Lady Macbeth in Shakespeare's Macbeth.
The part of James Bond was played by Sean Connery.
The role of Romeo was one of the most fulfilling I ever played.

senses.definition: to play the role of a character in a movie, play, etc.

examples.x: She portrayed the protagonist in the critically acclaimed film, earning praise for her nuanced performance.
He was chosen to portray the iconic historical figure in the epic biopic, showcasing his versatility as an actor.
The actor's ability to portray villains with depth and charisma has made him a sought-after talent in Hollywood.
She worked closely with the director to accurately portray the mannerisms and speech patterns of the real-life person she was portraying.

senses.definition: to act as a main character in a play, movie, etc.

examples.x: He starred in a critically acclaimed play at the local theater.
After years of hard work, she finally gets to star in a major motion picture.
The child actress stars in a popular TV show aimed at young audiences.
She stars as the protagonist in the new Broadway musical.
They hope to star in a big-budget production someday.

senses.definition: to learn and rehearse a role in a play or other performance as a substitute for the regular performer in case of need

examples.x: She was chosen to understudy the lead actress in case of illness or absence.
He spent weeks understudying the role of Hamlet in preparation for the big performance.
The director asked her to understudy several roles in case of last-minute changes.
The actor agreed to understudy the villain's role as a backup option.
The young actor eagerly accepted the chance to understudy the famous lead performer.

senses.definition: to indicate that characters leave the stage or scene
to assist someone by suggesting the next words or actions they may have forgotten or not fully learned

examples.x: During the play rehearsal, the director prompted the actor who had momentarily forgotten their lines.
The teacher prompted the shy student during the oral presentation, helping them recall key points they had prepared.
The supportive audience members would softly prompt the nervous singer.
The stage manager was ready to prompt any actor who needed assistance with their dialogue during the live performance.
In the acting workshop, participants took turns prompting each other to improvise.

senses.definition: to speak in a low, murmuring, and indistinct way, often with other people in the background making a similar noise, in order to create a background sound effect for a scene
to give a short performance in order to get a role in a movie, play, show, etc.

examples.x: She auditioned for the lead role in the play.
Hundreds of actors auditioned for the same part.
He auditioned but didn’t get the role he wanted.
She auditioned in front of a panel of judges.
They asked him to audition again with a different monologue.

senses.definition: to choose a performer to play a role in a movie, opera, play, etc.

examples.x: The director will cast the lead role in the upcoming musical next week.
They cast several actors from different backgrounds to bring diversity to the film.
The producer cast him as the villain due to his strong performance in auditions.
The theater company cast a famous actress for the main role in the play.

senses.definition: to assign the roles of a play, motion picture, etc. to unsuitable actors

examples.x: By the time they realized it, they had already miscast the key role.
The casting director is currently miscasting roles for the upcoming play.
If they had listened to the casting advice, they wouldn't have miscast the main character.
Critics argued that the director miscast the romantic lead, resulting in a lack of on-screen chemistry.
They miscast the supporting role, which affected the chemistry among the main characters.

senses.definition: cast again, in a different role
to practice a play, piece of music, etc. before the public performance

examples.x: The actors gathered in the theater to rehearse their lines and perfect their stage movements before the opening night.
The musicians decided to rehearse their new song several times to ensure a flawless performance.
Before the big dance recital, the ballet troupe spent hours rehearsing their routines.
The theater cast diligently rehearsed their scenes to deliver a polished and engaging performance.
The choir members dedicated extra time to rehearse their harmonies for the upcoming concert.

senses.definition: to assign the same type of role to an actor repeatedly
to give instructions to actors and organize the scenes or flow of a movie, play, etc.

examples.x: During rehearsals, the director focused on directing the actors' movements and expressions to convey the intended emotions.
She enjoys directing school plays because she gets to work closely with the young actors and help them improve.
The director spent hours on set, directing the actors to bring their characters to life.
She was hired to direct the actors in the upcoming theater production.
She directed the actors to experiment with different emotions to find the best delivery.

senses.definition: to change the original language of a movie or TV show into another language

examples.x: The studio hired professional voice actors to dub the foreign film into English for international audiences.
The television network plans to dub the popular Korean drama into Spanish to expand its audience base.
She was hired to dub the lead character in the Japanese anime series for its English release.
The film distributor decided to dub the classic French movie into Mandarin to appeal to the Chinese market.
The movie studio opted to dub the dialogue rather than use subtitles for the theatrical release.

senses.definition: to choose and arrange the parts that are crucial to the story of a movie, show, etc. and cut out unnecessary ones

examples.x: After shooting wrapped, the director and editor collaborated to edit the documentary.
The television producer hired an expert to edit the pilot episode.
In the editing room, the team worked tirelessly to edit the action sequences.
The independent filmmaker spent months editing her debut feature film.
The editor used advanced editing software to edit the comedy special.

senses.definition: to capture or record moving images, typically using a camera or video recording device

examples.x: She films short videos for her YouTube channel regularly.
Right now, the director is filming a crucial scene on set.
The team is filming the final episode of the television series.
By this time, they have already filmed three episodes of the new series.

senses.definition: to stop a movie or video to look at a particular frame

examples.x: During the film analysis class, the professor would frequently freeze the movie to discuss specific scenes in detail.
She paused the video player to freeze the frame showing the suspect entering the building, hoping to gather more clues.
The film critic would often freeze the movie at key moments to analyze the composition and cinematography.
In order to capture screenshots for their presentation, they had to freeze the video at precise moments.
The director instructed the editor to freeze the footage at the climax of the scene, emphasizing the dramatic tension.

senses.definition: to insert a camera shot between two scenes by cutting them
to provide money for and be in charge of the making of a movie, play, etc.

examples.x: She produced a TV series about adopted children.
The video was produced and directed by filmmaker Neil Campbell.
The wealthy philanthropist decided to produce a documentary on environmental conservation.
The renowned director secured funding from investors to produce a groundbreaking science fiction film.
The talented playwright was eager to produce her latest play.

senses.definition: to place the events of a play, movie, novel, etc. in a particular time or place

examples.x: The play is set in the roaring 1920s.
The novel is set during World War II.
The Director set the play in 19th century London.
The Playwright sets the scene in a busy marketplace.

senses.definition: to supervise the production of a play or theatrical performance
to make a movie, music, etc. available to the public
to show a video or film in a movie theater or on TV

examples.x: The cinema will screen the highly anticipated movie this weekend.
The television network will screen the documentary on environmental conservation next week.
The film festival will screen a selection of independent movies from various genres.
The local community center plans to screen classic films for residents every month.
The streaming service will screen the latest episodes of the popular TV series.

senses.definition: to change a book or play in a way that can be made into a movie, TV series, etc.

examples.x: The screenwriter adapted the bestselling novel into a screenplay.
The director worked closely with the playwright to adapt the stage production for television.
The producers hired a team of writers to adapt the classic play for a modern audience.
The screenwriter successfully adapted the nonfiction book into a gripping true-crime series.
The studio acquired the rights to adapt the graphic novel for TV.

senses.definition: to bring characters or objects to life through movement using animation techniques or computer programs

examples.x: The studio animates characters for its upcoming animated film using advanced CGI technology.
She animated a cute cartoon character for a children's television show.
He is animating a series of illustrations to create a dynamic storytelling experience.
The video game company animated lifelike movements for its characters to enhance gameplay.
She is animating a dancing figure for an online advertisement.

senses.definition: to take part in a play, TV show, movie, etc.

examples.x: The famous actor will appear in the upcoming blockbuster film.
She will appear as a guest judge on the reality TV show next week.
The beloved character will appear in the final season of the television series.
He is set to appear in a supporting role in the Broadway play next month.
The talk show host will appear as a guest star on the sitcom's season finale.

senses.definition: to add color to a black and white movie using computer techniques
to give a hint or signal to an actor or a performer to do or say something on the stage
to stop filming or recording
to turn a book, story, or an event into a movie or play

examples.x: Historical accounts of great leaders are often dramatized in biographical films to engage modern audiences.
The playwright decided to dramatize the novel, adapting it into an engaging and visually stunning stage production.
The acclaimed director was hired to dramatize the bestselling novel into a blockbuster film.
The playwright took creative liberties to dramatize the historical event, bringing it to life on stage.
The producers decided to dramatize the true crime story for television, capturing the public's attention with its gripping narrative.

senses.definition: to act a role in a motion picture or perform a play on stage

examples.x: She will enact the lead role in the upcoming romantic comedy.
He enacted the role of a troubled detective in last year's acclaimed thriller.
The actors rehearsed tirelessly to enact their parts in the historical drama.
They are currently enacting scenes from a famous play in drama class.
During the audition, she was enacting a dramatic monologue that impressed the casting director.

senses.definition: to give a performance of something such as a play or a piece of music for entertainment

examples.x: The band will perform at the city park next week.
The comedian performed in front of a large audience.
The magician performed an incredible trick.
The orchestra will perform Beethoven's Fifth Symphony tonight.
They perform a traditional dance at the festival every year.

senses.definition: to stage a play, a show, etc. for an audience

examples.x: They are putting on a musical at the local theater next month.

senses.definition: to film or take a photograph of something
to present a play or other event to an audience

examples.x: The theater company stages Shakespearean plays throughout the year.
They stage a musical production every spring.
The theater group plans to stage a new production of Romeo and Juliet this summer.
The director is known for staging elaborate and visually stunning plays.
The opera will be staged at the historic downtown theater.

senses.definition: to adjust the lens of a camera in a way that makes the person or thing being filmed or photographed appear closer or larger

examples.x: The photographer zoomed in to capture the intricate details of the butterfly's wings.

senses.definition: to adjust the lens of a camera in a way that makes the person or thing being filmed or photographed appear further away or smaller

examples.x: The photographer skillfully zoomed out, revealing the breathtaking expanse of the sunset.

senses.definition: (of a play, motion picture, or new product) to fail to be of any success or produce the intended effect

examples.x: Despite high expectations, the Broadway play flopped after receiving unfavorable reviews.
The ambitious film, with its star-studded cast, unexpectedly flopped at the box office.
The comedy show, once popular, began to flop in its later seasons.
Despite heavy marketing efforts, the fashion brand's new collection flopped.
After a series of successful albums, the artist's latest release unexpectedly flopped.

senses.definition: to publicly praise a new book, motion picture, etc. as a way of promoting it

examples.x: The author went on multiple talk shows to plug her latest novel and discuss its themes.
During the press conference, the director plugged the upcoming film, highlighting its innovative cinematography.
Celebrities often plug products on their social media platforms to endorse them to their followers.
The actor made appearances on late-night television to plug his new movie and engage with fans.
The comedian plugged his upcoming stand-up special, promising audiences an evening of laughter and entertainment.

senses.definition: to watch a movie, play, TV show, etc. in advance of public presentation

examples.x: Critics are invited to preview the new movie before its official release.
She previews the next episode of her favorite TV series online.
The director previews the final cut of the film before its premiere.
Journalists are allowed to preview the new video game before it hits the shelves.
He previews the latest musical on Broadway to write a review for the newspaper.

senses.definition: to convey a story or message using only body movements and facial expressions, without the use of words
(in a play, live performance, etc.) to forget or make errors in the delivery of one's dialogue
